:::{#lem-ph-which-subject}
#### The which-subject-fails factor

The which-subject-fails factor of @eq-ph-chain-rule equals the
partial-likelihood contribution $\Lik^*_i(\vb)$ of @def-ph-partial-lik:
the shared baseline factor cancels, leaving

$$
\Pr\sb{(i) \text{ fails} \mid 1 \text{ failure at } t_i,\ R(t_i)}
\approx
\frac{\red{\hazfactor(\vx_{(i)})}}{\red{S_i}}
= \Lik^*_i(\vb).
$$ {#eq-ph-which-subject}
:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

::: {.proof}
Solve @eq-ph-chain-rule for the which-subject factor and substitute
the numerator $q_{(i)}(t_i)$ from @lem-ph-single-subj-fail
and the denominator $\blue{\haz_0(t_i)\,\Delta t}\,\red{S_i}$ from @lem-ph-whether-failure;
the shared $\blue{\haz_0(t_i)\,\Delta t}$ cancels between numerator and denominator:

$$
\ba
\Pr\sb{(i) \text{ fails} \mid 1 \text{ failure at } t_i,\ R(t_i)}
&= \frac{\Pr\sb{(i) \text{ fails at } t_i \mid R(t_i)}}
         {\Pr\sb{1 \text{ failure at } t_i \mid R(t_i)}}
&& \text{(rearrange @eq-ph-chain-rule)}
\\
&\approx \frac{q^*_{(i)}(t_i)}{\blue{\haz_0(t_i)\,\Delta t}\;\red{S_i}}
&& \text{(@lem-ph-single-subj-fail and @lem-ph-whether-failure)}
\\
&= \frac{\blue{\haz_0(t_i)\,\Delta t}\;\red{\hazfactor(\vx_{(i)})}}
         {\blue{\haz_0(t_i)\,\Delta t}\;\red{S_i}}
&& \text{(definition of } q^*_{(i)}(t_i) \text{)}
\\
&= \frac{\red{\hazfactor(\vx_{(i)})}}{\red{S_i}}
&& \text{(cancel the shared } \blue{\haz_0(t_i)\,\Delta t} \text{)}
\\
&= \Lik^*_i(\vb).
&& \text{(definition of } \Lik^*_i(\vb),\ \text{@def-ph-partial-lik)}
\ea
$$

The final $=$ is *exact*: the $\blue{\haz_0(t_i)\,\Delta t}$ factors cancel algebraically,
leaving a ratio independent of $\Delta t$,
so the which-subject factor equals the partial-likelihood contribution $\Lik^*_i(\vb)$
free of both the baseline hazard and the grid width.
:::

:::{#exm-ph-which-subject}
#### The which-subject factor in the three-subject risk set

Continue the running scenario of @exm-ph-single-subj-fail,
with subject $2$ failing at $t_i = 4$ and $\red{S_i} = 6$ (@exm-ph-whether-failure).
By @eq-ph-which-subject,

$$
\ba
\Lik^*_i(\vb)
&= \frac{\red{\hazfactor(\vx_2)}}{\red{S_i}}
&& \text{(@eq-ph-which-subject)}
\\
&= \frac{2}{6}
&& \text{(substitute } \hazfactor(\vx_2) = 2,\ S_i = 6\text{)}
\\
&= \frac{1}{3} ,
&& \text{(arithmetic)}
\ea
$$

independent of the baseline $\blue{\haz_0(4)}$ and the grid width $\Delta t$ —
the cancellation that the partial likelihood relies on.
This value $\tfrac{1}{3}$ matches the one pinned by the chain rule in @exm-ph-event-chain-rule.
:::
